agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "New 'Express Delivery' product offering time-sensitive delivery with a sub-2-hour window, premium pricing, and a refund policy for missed delivery windows. Spans order placement, driver assignment, real-time fulfillment, and post-delivery refund processing.",
    "bounded_context": "Express Fulfillment — covers eligibility determination, express order lifecycle, driver qualification, SLA tracking, and express-specific refund processing. Intersects with: Driver Management (ratings), Warehouse/Inventory (proximity), Billing (fees/refunds), and Customer Communication (tracking).",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Express Delivery", "definition": "A premium delivery option with a target fulfillment window of 2 hours from order placement, available to eligible customers for an additional fee." },
      { "term": "Express Fee", "definition": "The $12.99 surcharge applied on top of the base delivery fee when a customer selects express delivery." },
      { "term": "Base Delivery Fee", "definition": "The standard delivery fee that applies to all orders regardless of express selection. Not subject to express refund." },
      { "term": "Express Window", "definition": "The 2-hour period from order placement within which express delivery should be completed." },
      { "term": "Pickup SLA", "definition": "The 15-minute internal target for a driver to physically pick up an express order from the warehouse after placement." },
      { "term": "Warehouse Proximity", "definition": "The maximum 10km radius from a warehouse within which a customer's delivery address must fall to be eligible for express delivery." },
      { "term": "Express-Qualified Driver", "definition": "A driver with a rating of at least 4.0 stars who is eligible to fulfill express orders." },
      { "term": "Driver Replacement Window", "definition": "The 10-minute period after a driver cancels mid-delivery within which a replacement driver must be assigned before an automatic refund is triggered." },
      { "term": "Missed Window Refund", "definition": "A goodwill refund of the express fee (not the base delivery fee) issued when the express window is not met, subject to exclusions (e.g., wrong customer address)." },
      { "term": "Wrong Address Exclusion", "definition": "A refund exclusion condition where the customer provided an incorrect delivery address, voiding the express window refund." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "A customer may only select express delivery if their delivery address is within 10km of an active warehouse.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Physical constraint — if the distance exceeds 10km, the 2-hour window is physically unachievable. Allowing ineligible orders would guarantee SLA failures.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a delivery address >10km from all warehouses, the system MUST NOT allow express delivery selection."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "Only drivers with a rating of 4.0 stars or above may be assigned to express orders.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Business policy — the rating threshold is a quality lever that operations could adjust (e.g., to 3.5 in driver-scarce markets). Not a physical or data-integrity constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a driver with a rating below 4.0, the system MUST NOT assign them to an express order."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "Express orders must be picked up by a driver within 15 minutes of order placement.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Operational target — the 15-minute window is tunable based on market conditions. However, breaching it signals the 2-hour window is at risk.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given an express order not picked up within 15 minutes, the system MUST raise an operational alert/escalation."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "The express fee is $12.99, charged in addition to the base delivery fee.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Pricing policy — can be changed by Finance or a pricing team at any time.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a customer selects express delivery, the order total MUST include both the base delivery fee and a $12.99 express surcharge as separate line items."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "When the express window is missed, only the express fee is refunded — never the base delivery fee.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Financial integrity — the base delivery fee covers the cost of goods movement that still occurred. Refunding it would mean subsidizing a completed service.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a missed express window triggering a refund, the refund amount MUST equal the express fee only, and the base delivery fee MUST remain charged."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "No express fee refund is issued if the customer provided an incorrect delivery address.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Data integrity / moral hazard — the SLA failure was caused by the customer, not the platform. Refunding would create a perverse incentive.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a missed express window where the delivery address was flagged as incorrect by the driver, the system MUST NOT issue an express fee refund."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "If a driver cancels mid-delivery and no replacement is assigned within 10 minutes, the express fee is automatically refunded.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Customer success policy — the 10-minute window and auto-refund trigger are tunable business decisions.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a driver cancellation on an express order with no replacement assigned within 10 minutes, the system MUST automatically initiate an express fee refund."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "Express delivery customers must have access to real-time driver tracking from the moment a driver is assigned.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Product feature requirement — important for UX but not a data or domain integrity constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given an express order with an assigned driver, the customer-facing app MUST display real-time driver location."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Customer is exactly 10km from the nearest warehouse", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — need to clarify if 10km is inclusive (<=10km) or exclusive (<10km). Recommend inclusive." },
    { "scenario": "Driver rating drops below 4.0 while en route on an express delivery", "expected_behavior": "The delivery in progress should NOT be interrupted. Rating check applies at assignment time only." },
    { "scenario": "Pickup SLA breached (>15 min) but delivery still completed within 2 hours", "expected_behavior": "No refund triggered — the express window was met. The pickup SLA breach is an internal operational metric, not a customer-facing commitment." },
    { "scenario": "Driver cancels at minute 118 of a 120-minute window", "expected_behavior": "Replacement window (10 min) would exceed the express window. Both the replacement-failure refund AND the missed-window refund could trigger — clarify if these are the same refund or could stack." },
    { "scenario": "Customer changes address after order placement to one >10km from warehouse", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — should express be canceled? Should the customer be warned? Should the SLA be voided?" },
    { "scenario": "Wrong address dispute — customer claims address was correct, driver disagrees", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — who adjudicates? Need a dispute resolution process. Without it, the wrong-address exclusion (BR-6) is unenforceable." },
    { "scenario": "All express-qualified drivers are occupied when an express order is placed", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — should the system reject the express order, queue it (risking the 15-min pickup SLA), or downgrade to standard?" },
    { "scenario": "Refund already processed but address later confirmed correct", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — is there a reversal process? Can a denied refund be reopened?" },
    { "scenario": "Multiple warehouse coverage — customer is within 10km of two warehouses", "expected_behavior": "UNRESOLVED — which warehouse fulfills? Nearest? Most stocked? This affects pickup SLA feasibility." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Express fee ($12.99) creates a new revenue stream but exposes the company to refund liability on every missed window. Refund volume is directly tied to operational execution quality. The wrong-address exclusion is a critical cost-control mechanism but requires reliable adjudication to avoid both fraud and customer churn.",
    "operational": "Constraining express to 4-star drivers reduces the eligible driver pool significantly — this creates capacity risk in low-supply markets. The 15-minute pickup SLA requires near-real-time dispatch optimization. The 10-minute replacement window demands a hot-standby or rapid-reassignment capability that may not exist today.",
    "user": "Real-time tracking and a prominent 2-hour commitment set high expectations. The contradiction between 'guarantee' (PM/CS) and 'best effort' (Legal) creates a UX messaging risk — if marketing says 'guaranteed' but terms say 'best effort,' trust erodes on the first missed delivery.",
    "risk": "Legal exposure if 'guarantee' language is used in customer-facing materials. Operational risk if driver supply cannot meet express demand. Financial risk if refund rate exceeds projections due to systemic SLA misses. Reputational risk if the wrong-address exclusion is perceived as a loophole to avoid refunds.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Customers", "impact": "New premium option with time-sensitive value proposition. High expectations set by 'guarantee' language. Refund exclusions (wrong address) may feel adversarial without clear communication." },
      { "group": "Drivers", "impact": "Only 4-star+ drivers eligible — creates a two-tier driver system. Express orders likely have tighter time pressure, potentially affecting driver satisfaction and safety." },
      { "group": "Warehouse Operations", "impact": "15-minute pickup SLA means express orders must be prioritized in pick/pack workflows. May require dedicated express staging areas." },
      { "group": "Customer Support", "impact": "Will handle wrong-address disputes, missed-window complaints, and refund inquiries. Need clear adjudication rules and tooling." },
      { "group": "Finance", "impact": "New revenue line item, new refund category, 3-business-day processing commitment. Need reconciliation between auto-refunds (BR-7) and manual refund processing." },
      { "group": "Legal/Compliance", "impact": "Must review all customer-facing language to ensure no enforceable guarantee is created. Refund policy must be clearly disclosed as goodwill." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 38,
    "reasoning": "The spec contains a fundamental unresolved contradiction between PM ('guaranteed') and Legal ('best effort / not a contractual guarantee') that affects every customer-facing touchpoint, refund policy framing, and acceptance criteria. Additionally, the auto-refund trigger (BR-7, Customer Success) conflicts with the 3-business-day processing timeline (Finance), and there is no clarity on who adjudicates wrong-address disputes. Multiple critical operational gaps (driver supply, warehouse selection, address changes) are unaddressed. Cannot confidently define acceptance criteria until the guarantee-vs-best-effort contradiction is resolved.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "10km warehouse proximity as an eligibility constraint",
      "Express fee as a separate line item from base delivery fee",
      "Only express fee (not base fee) is refundable on missed window",
      "Wrong address voids the refund"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Whether the 2-hour window is a guarantee or best-effort SLA — this changes everything",
      "Refund processing: automatic vs 3-business-day processing — contradictory signals",
      "Wrong-address adjudication process — completely undefined",
      "Driver supply constraints and what happens when no express driver is available",
      "How replacement driver assignment interacts with the express window clock"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The 10km distance is calculated as straight-line (geodesic) distance, not road distance — needs confirmation",
      "Driver rating is a rolling average, not per-delivery — needs confirmation",
      "The 2-hour window starts at order placement time, not at pickup time",
      "The express fee is fixed, not dynamic based on distance or demand",
      "A single warehouse fulfills each express order (no multi-warehouse routing)"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "PM and Customer Success describe the 2-hour window as a 'guarantee,' while Legal explicitly states it is a 'best effort SLA' and that refunds are 'goodwill gestures, not legally enforceable.' This is not a nuance — it is a fundamental contradiction that affects: (1) all customer-facing language, (2) whether refunds are obligatory or discretionary, (3) potential legal liability. The product cannot ship with both positions.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Legal/Compliance", "Customer Support", "Marketing"],
      "options": [
        "A: Treat as best-effort with goodwill refund — customer-facing language says 'target' not 'guarantee,' refunds are discretionary",
        "B: Treat as a commercial guarantee with automatic refund — accept the financial exposure, but add clear terms limiting legal liability",
        "C: Hybrid — use 'guarantee' in marketing but define it in T&C as 'best effort with automatic goodwill refund' (common industry pattern)"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C is standard in the courier industry (e.g., Domino's '30 minutes or free' evolved this way). But Legal MUST approve the specific language. This must be resolved before any acceptance criteria can be defined."
    },
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "Customer Success says refunds should be 'automatic' when a replacement driver isn't assigned within 10 minutes. Finance says refunds are 'processed within 3 business days.' These cannot both be true for the same refund event. Is the refund initiated automatically but processed in 3 days, or is there a manual review step?",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Finance", "Customer Support"],
      "options": [
        "A: Auto-initiate refund immediately, process/settle within 3 business days (refund is instant from customer's perspective but hits the ledger later)",
        "B: Auto-flag for refund, but require manual approval within 3 business days",
        "C: Fully automatic end-to-end for replacement-failure refunds, 3-day processing only for missed-window refunds"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A — auto-initiate but process within 3 business days. This is standard payment processing. The customer sees 'refund issued,' Finance sees it in the next settlement batch. Needs Finance sign-off."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The 'wrong address' exclusion (Finance) has no defined adjudication process. Who determines whether an address is 'wrong'? The driver? The system via geocoding? A support agent? Without this, BR-6 is unenforceable and will generate customer disputes.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Customer Support", "Drivers"],
      "options": [
        "A: Driver flags wrong address in-app with photo evidence, support reviews within 24 hours",
        "B: System validates address at order time via geocoding — if validated, wrong-address exclusion cannot apply",
        "C: Hybrid — validate at order time, but allow driver to flag discrepancies with evidence"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C — validate upfront to prevent most issues, but allow driver flagging as a safety net. This is the industry standard approach."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No stakeholder addressed what happens when no express-qualified driver is available at order time. This is the most likely real-world failure mode. Should the system: reject the express option, queue and hope, or transparently tell the customer?",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Operations", "Drivers"],
      "options": [
        "A: Hide the express option when no driver is available within estimated pickup radius",
        "B: Accept the order and escalate to dispatch — risk missing the 15-minute pickup SLA",
        "C: Accept but show 'express may be delayed' warning"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A — do not offer what you cannot fulfill. This is the only option consistent with either a 'guarantee' or a credible 'best effort' positioning."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No one defined what happens to the express window clock when a driver cancels mid-delivery. Does the 2-hour window reset? Pause during the 10-minute replacement window? Continue running? This directly affects whether a refund is triggered.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Operations", "Finance"],
      "options": [
        "A: Clock continues — the 2-hour window is absolute from order placement",
        "B: Clock pauses during replacement search (up to 10 minutes)",
        "C: Clock resets from replacement driver assignment"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A — from the customer's perspective, they ordered at time T and expect delivery by T+2h. Internal driver issues are not the customer's problem. This is the only customer-centric interpretation."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": []
}
